description: JLVM ZK / auth-DB opcode cross-language test vectors. Scala (metakit) is the reference; Rust (jlvm-core) must reproduce every expected value byte-for-byte. Same structure and differential-runner contract as json_logic_test_vectors.json. The 'known_answer' category carries independent ground truth (circomlib poseidon, real SP1 groth16 fixture, RFC-9381 ECVRF-EDWARDS25519, EIP-197 pairing identity, and the published eth2/IETF BLS vectors from ethereum/bls12-381-tests v0.1.2). bls_verify / bls_aggregate_verify use the eth2 ProofOfPossession ciphersuite BLS_SIG_BLS12381G2_XMD:SHA-256_SSWU_RO_POP_ (minimal-pubkey-size: 48B G1 pubkeys, 96B G2 signatures), generated by running the metakit Bls12381 primitive (BouncyCastle 1.85); the earlier MIRACL-SVDW deferral is resolved. ERROR CONVENTION: a case with "error": true (and NO "expected") asserts that evaluation MUST FAIL in every implementation (the Scala reference raises a JsonLogicException or throws); all other cases carry "expected" and must reproduce it byte-for-byte.

cases.note: 4-input poseidon (max width t=5 ⇒ 4 inputs is the largest supported)
5-input poseidon — over max width (t=6 > MaxWidth=5): capture Scala behavior
non-canonical Fr (all-ones = 2^256-1 > R) — rejected by parseFr
non-canonical Fr (value == R exactly) — must be rejected (canonical requires < R)
wrong-width hex (4 bytes, not 32)
empty hex input (0 bytes, not 32)
odd-length hex body (63 nibbles) — even-length required
uppercase hex nibble (A) — codec is lowercase-only
empty input list — poseidon requires at least one element
tampered leaf (different leaf, correct root+siblings) ⇒ false
wrong index: correct leaf+siblings for pos 42 but index 170 — exercises LSB left/right bits ⇒ false
out-of-range index (== 2^depth, depth=8) — must be rejected
deeper-sibling tamper (top/root-most sibling changed) ⇒ false
non-canonical leaf hex (all-ones > R) — rejected
malformed leaf hex (non-hex / wrong width) — rejected
arg-order discriminator: root and leaf swapped ⇒ false (proves arg order matters)
inclusion proof for keyA -> valid:true, included:true, value 'alice'
absence (Default witness: empty slot) -> valid:true, included:false, value null
absence (OtherLeaf witness: a different leaf occupies the path) -> valid:true, included:false, value null
valid inclusion proof against a wrong (zero) root -> valid:false
inclusion proof value bytes tampered (valueDigest untouched) -> ValueBindingFailed -> valid:false
discriminating: a valid proof for keyB returns ITS key/value ('bob'), not keyA's
a non-decodable proof object -> Result error
non-hex root string -> Result error
valid inclusion proof for a1->v-a1 -> true
correct key/proof but wrong claimed value -> false
valid proof against a wrong root -> false
proof attests a1 but key b7 is queried (path mismatch) -> false
discriminating: a2->v-a2 with a2's own proof verifies -> true
a non-decodable proof object -> Result error
the COMPLETE entry set under prefix ab verifies -> true
dropping ab56 breaks completeness (claimed key-set != attested path-set) -> false
altering ab34's value breaks value-binding -> false
adding an extra out-of-set entry breaks completeness -> false
a non-decodable batch proof object -> Result error
g1 + (-g1) == point at infinity (all-zero encoding)
P + infinity == P (additive identity; second operand is the all-zero point at infinity)
off-curve point (1,1) not on y^2=x^3+3 — Scala raises JsonLogicException
wrong-width G1 point (2 bytes, not 64)
mul(g1, 0) == point at infinity (all-zero encoding)
off-curve point (1,1) not on y^2=x^3+3 — Scala raises JsonLogicException
wrong-width G1 point (2 bytes, not 64)
wrong-width scalar (2 bytes, not 32)
empty pairing product is the identity ⇒ true
wrong-width G2 point (2 bytes, not 128)
off-curve G1 point (1,1) paired with the canonical G2 generator — Scala raises JsonLogicException
tampered gamma (first proof byte flipped) ⇒ invalid ⇒ {valid:false, beta:null}
tampered scalar s (last proof byte flipped; gamma stays on-curve) ⇒ equation fails ⇒ {valid:false, beta:null}
wrong-width pk (31 bytes, not 32) — Scala raises JsonLogicException
wrong-width proof (79 bytes, not 80) — Scala raises JsonLogicException
tampered s (last proof byte flipped; R stays on-curve) ⇒ well-formed but wrong ⇒ false
tampered R (first byte of R flipped — moves R off-curve) — capture Scala behavior
off-curve pk (1,1) not on y^2=x^3+3 — capture Scala behavior (error or false?)
point-at-infinity pk (0,0) — on-curve by convention; capture Scala behavior
wrong-width proof (2 bytes, not 96)
non-lowercase (uppercase) proof hex — codec is lowercase-only
KNOWN-ANSWER: real SP1 Groth16-BN254 proof (circuit v6.1.0) for {"if":[{">":[{"var":"amount"},100]},"premium","standard"]} on {"amount":150} -> "premium"; anchor fixture sp1-groth16-premium.json
tampered proof: lowest bit of last (356th) proof byte flipped -> Groth16 pairing fails
tampered proof: lowest bit of proof byte 10 flipped (inside first proof field element) -> pairing fails
wrong publicValues: lowest bit of byte 0 flipped -> Groth16 public-input commitment changes -> pairing fails
wrong verifier selector: lowest bit of proof byte 0 flipped (4-byte SP1 selector) -> selector mismatch -> false
wrong programVKey: lowest bit of byte 0 flipped -> Groth16 input[0] changes -> pairing fails
wrong-width vkey (31 bytes, not 32) -> Scala raises JsonLogicException "groth16_verify vkey: expected 32 bytes, got 31"
wrong-width vkey (33 bytes, not 32) -> Scala raises JsonLogicException "groth16_verify vkey: expected 32 bytes, got 33"
self-generated: valid single verify (sk seed=101) -> true
self-generated: wrong message (valid sig over a different msg) -> false
self-generated: wrong public key (another signer pk) -> false
self-generated: tampered signature (last byte flipped) -> false
self-generated: wrong-width public key (47 bytes, not 48) -> error
self-generated: wrong-width signature (95 bytes, not 96) -> error
self-generated: same-message aggregate of N=4 signers -> true
self-generated: one bad signer (last pk swapped for a non-signer) -> false
self-generated: aggregate verified against the wrong message -> false
self-generated: aggregate with a wrong-width pubkey (47 bytes) -> error
self-generated: aggregate with a wrong-width aggregate signature (95 bytes) -> error
KNOWN-ANSWER (eth2 verify_valid_case_e8a50c445c855360, ethereum/bls12-381-tests v0.1.2): privkey1, msg=0x00*32 -> true
KNOWN-ANSWER (eth2 verify_valid_case_195246ee3bd3b6ec == tessellation-bls canonical KAT): privkey3, msg=0xab*32 -> true
KNOWN-ANSWER (eth2 verify_wrong_pubkey_case_2f09d443ab8a3ac2): privkey1 sig under privkey2 pubkey -> false
KNOWN-ANSWER (eth2 fast_aggregate_verify_valid_3d7576f3c0e3570a): 3 pubkeys, msg=0xab*32 -> true
KNOWN-ANSWER (eth2 fast_aggregate_verify_extra_pubkey_5a38e6b4017fe4dd): extra 4th pubkey for a 3-signer aggregate -> false
